title: Good context = smallest set of highest-signal tokens

body.fact: Good context engineering means finding the smallest possible set of high-signal tokens that maximize the likelihood of the desired outcome. Minimal does not mean short — you still need sufficient information for the agent to adhere to desired behavior. But every unnecessary token depletes the model's finite attention budget with diminishing marginal returns.
body.context: This is the guiding principle for all context engineering decisions: system prompts, tool design, example selection, and conversation history management. It applies because transformer attention creates n² pairwise relationships between tokens — more tokens means each relationship gets less attention.
